task_completion.description: Load the Landsat multispectral image into ESA SNAP Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ESA SNAP Desktop is open and 'landsat_multispectral.tif' is visible in the Product Explorer or loaded into the main view.

task_completion.description: Configure a false-color composite using the specified bands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Open RGB Image Window' dialog or layer properties show Red assigned to 'band_1', Green assigned to 'band_2', and Blue assigned to 'band_3', resulting in a false-color image displayed in the viewer.

task_completion.description: Include a scale bar in the figure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scale bar graphic is clearly visible overlaid on the satellite image in the SNAP image view.

task_completion.description: Include a north arrow in the figure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A north arrow graphic is clearly visible overlaid on the satellite image in the SNAP image view.

task_completion.description: Export the rendered map as a PNG to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog shows the file being saved as '/home/ga/snap_exports/landsat_figure.png' with the PNG format selected, or a terminal/file manager confirms the creation of this specific file.

integrity.description: The agent used the ESA SNAP Desktop GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the ESA SNAP graphical interface (menus, dialogs, toolbars) rather than relying solely on command-line tools like 'gpt' or 'gdal' to generate the output.

integrity.description: The scale bar and north arrow were added using SNAP's built-in mapping tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses SNAP's layer manager or mapping tools to add the scale bar and north arrow, rather than opening the exported image in an external image editor (like GIMP) to paste them in.

integrity.description: The exported image is a genuine render of the loaded data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final image matches the false-color composite seen in the SNAP viewer, rather than being a downloaded stock image or a blank canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at /home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif. The expected output is a valid PNG image saved exactly at /home/ga/snap_exports/landsat_figure.png with minimum dimensions of 400x400 pixels. The final image must be a false-color composite (Red=band_1, Green=band_2, Blue=band_3) and visually contain both a scale bar and a north arrow.
